description_long: This object is a vintage wall-mounted corded telephone, featuring a rectangular wooden base with a dark, polished finish. The front panel displays two prominent brass bells at the top, a rotary dial in the center, and a metal hook supporting a separate earpiece and mouthpiece connected by a coiled black cord. The earpiece and mouthpiece are cylindrical, with metallic and wooden accents, and are attached to the left side of the base. The rotary dial is made of brass and has finger holes for dialing numbers. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century telephones commonly found in homes or offices, with visible screws and hardware adding to its antique character.
description: A vintage wall-mounted corded telephone with a wooden base, brass rotary dial, and a separate handset connected by a coiled cord.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden base, two brass bells, rotary dial, and the handset hanging on the left side.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the slim profile and the protruding handset with its coiled cord.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain wooden back and the side-mounted handset.
description_view_3: Right side view focusing on the cylindrical handset and the coiled cord, with the rotary dial partially visible.
